response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with a strong background in web infrastructure, low-latency services, and developer tooling, but his profile has no meaningful overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator workload analysis, hardware-software co-design, and GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture — none of which appear in his resume. His closest relevant experience is low-latency DLang work at Sociomantic and some C/C++ component work at National Instruments, but neither approaches the hardware modeling or deep-learning performance optimization required at principal IC level.
strengths: Long career (~17 years) demonstrating consistent technical growth across multiple domains including embedded and systems-level work
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fiber-based concurrency) shows some performance engineering mindset
C and C++ experience at National Instruments with measurement software provides a thin hardware-adjacent foundation
concerns: Zero evidence of SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or chip-level performance analysis — the core technical requirement of the role
No AI/ML or deep-learning workload experience whatsoever; the role requires principal-level expertise in DNN training and inference optimization
No exposure to GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD pipelines, or AI accelerator design — all key preferred qualifications that a strong candidate would hold

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware modeling experience
